description: Key-fingerprint encoding test vectors (RFC-ACDP-0010 §6): key_fingerprint = 'sha256:' + lowercase_hex(SHA-256(raw_public_key_bytes)), where raw bytes are the 32-byte raw Ed25519 public key or the 33-byte SEC1-compressed P-256 point (0x02/0x03 parity prefix + 32-byte big-endian x). The input is the key bytes alone — no multicodec prefix, no DER/SPKI framing, no base64/multibase, no JWK serialization. Divergence here is the receipt-layer equivalent of the timestamp-precision bug. Executed arithmetically by scripts/conformance-runner.py.

vectors.name: Ed25519 — sig-001 test producer key (raw 32 bytes)
Ed25519 — rcpt-001 registry receipt key (raw 32 bytes)
ECDSA-P256 — sig-002 test key (private scalar = 1; SEC1 compressed point, 33 bytes)

uncompressed_point.note: The DID document publishes the uncompressed (x, y) JWK pair (registries/signature-algorithms.md); the verifier MUST compress before fingerprinting: prefix 0x03 because y is odd.
